notes: Anchored to .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Long-idle live session. Daemon ran with IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so the 20 s post-turn-1 sleep cleanly exceeds the TTL (the production default 30 min would force a 32+ min recording). PID liveness check holds the session through the idle window — no transcript_removed for the UUID, state stays ready, turn 2 resumes normally.
pre-session row appears in ready after claude launch
UUID transcript materializes on first user input
first turn ends; session enters its idle window

Expected: "Session does NOT disappear, state remains ready" — turn 2 starts ~18.9 s after turn 1 ended, well past the 15 s readyTTL. The session survived because PID liveness held it; the very fact that turn 2's working transition fires on the SAME UUID proves the session was never swept.
Expected: "On the next user prompt: ready → working resumes normally" — turn 2 completes cleanly with the same UUID, no flicker.
driver's tmux kill at end of script — out of scope for this scenario but recorded for completeness.
